title: Hydropark Widget — slider / stepper (numeric scalar input)
description: The `slider` / `stepper` widget (SPEC 9.4, P1-06.4). One contract, two first-party PRESENTATIONS of the same numeric-scalar input — a continuous `slider` (track + thumb) and a discrete `stepper` (value field with −/＋ buttons) — mirroring how `segmented_toggle` / `switch` share one library entry. The manifest selects the presentation via `type`; both are members of `_widget-base.schema.json#/$defs/knownWidgetTypeV1`. Purpose: capture a bounded number (servings, portions, temperature, grams) with `min`/`max`/`step` and bind it two-way to a `scalar<number>` shared-state slot (SPEC 8.3.4). This schema `allOf`-extends the base envelope, pins `type`, closes `props` (additionalProperties:false), tightens `emits` to this widget's event set, and — in the non-normative `x-hydropark-widget` block — records the facets JSON Schema cannot validate against the manifest alone: default region/priority/event set, the accepted slot kind, the typed runtime state, the loading/empty/error/read-only behavior, and the mechanical a11y contract enforced by the X-A11Y gate (SPEC 9.10, contract §8).

type.description: Pinned to one of the two presentations of this widget, both drawn from knownWidgetTypeV1. `slider` renders a continuous track (role=slider); `stepper` renders a value field with decrement/increment buttons (role=spinbutton). An older library that lacks the chosen type degrades to the placeholder, never a validation failure (SPEC 9.8).

binds_state.description: REQUIRED for this widget: the `scalar<number>` slot this control renders and (iff its owning skill is the slot's writer-of-record) edits (SPEC 8.3.4). The slot's kind is declared in `shared_state`; the fit `scalar(number)` is a cross-artifact certification check (P1-04.5), see x-hydropark-widget.binds_state. When the owning skill is NOT the writer-of-record the binding resolves to read-only at merge (contract §5): the control renders live but every edit affordance is disabled and the writer is named.

props.description: Typed, immutable-per-instance configuration for the numeric input. Closed set (additionalProperties:false).

min.description: Minimum selectable value (inclusive). Maps to aria-valuemin and the Home key.

max.description: Maximum selectable value (inclusive). Maps to aria-valuemax and the End key. MUST be strictly greater than `min` — a cross-field invariant JSON Schema cannot express in-envelope; enforced by certification (P1-04.5).

step.description: Granularity of one increment: Arrow-key nudge, −/＋ button press, and slider snap. SHOULD evenly divide (max − min) so both endpoints are reachable; certification warns when it does not.

page_step.description: Larger jump for PageUp / PageDown (and modifier-augmented steps). Omitted → the widget uses max(step, round((max − min) / 10)).

default_value.description: Value shown before the bound slot holds one (drives the empty → populated transition). MUST lie within [min, max] and align to `step` measured from `min`; certification checks. Omitted → the widget shows the mandatory empty state until the slot or a tool sets a value. Presence NEVER auto-writes the slot — display-only until the user (or a tool) commits a value, so a non-writer cannot smuggle a write (contract §5).

unit_label.description: Unit suffix rendered after the value and folded into aria-valuetext so the reading is announced with its unit ('4 servings', '180 g'), never a bare number (WCAG — meaningful text, not glyph-only). Localizable (SPEC 8.7).

decimals.description: Fixed decimal places for the DISPLAYED and announced value. Omitted → inferred from `step` (an integer step yields 0). Formatting only; the stored scalar keeps full precision.

show_value.description: Render the live numeric readout beside the control. When false the value is still exposed to assistive tech via aria-valuenow / aria-valuetext — it is never hidden from screen readers, only from the visual layout.

marks.description: `slider` presentation only: render non-interactive tick marks at each `step`, with text labels at min and max (never hue-only). Ignored by the `stepper` presentation.

clamp.description: Governs a bound value delivered OUTSIDE [min, max] (e.g. written by another skill's tool). true → clamp into range for display and flag the control out-of-range with text + icon (not color-only); false → surface the mandatory error state instead. Either way the widget never crashes on an out-of-range slot value.

commit_on.description: When a `slider` drag writes the bound scalar: `release` writes once on pointer/keyboard release (default — avoids a write storm on the bus and keeps the deterministic UI-first path, SPEC 9.3 #2); `change` writes on every intermediate value. The `stepper` presentation always commits once per discrete step and ignores this field.

emits.description: This widget's event set (SPEC 9.3 #4). Recognized names: `value_changed` (fires on each committed value; default to_chat=false — a servings tweak must not spam the transcript) and `limit_reached` (fires when the user tries to step past min/max; default to_chat=false). Omitted → normalization fills both with their default to_chat. Certification rejects duplicate names. Posting an event appends to the transcript / fires an OS notification only when to_chat/time-critical; it NEVER auto-runs inference (SPEC 9.3 #4).

emittedEvent.description: An emitted event, tightened to this widget's known names (a per-widget narrowing of _widget-base.schema.json#/$defs/eventDecl). Bare string uses the widget-type's default to_chat; the object form overrides it.

to_chat.description: Override the event's default to_chat. Both events default to false.

runtimeState.description: NON-MANIFEST (documentation only). The widget's live projection, produced by the bound slot + tools + user input and held by the renderer (SPEC 9.2 'typed runtime state'; 9.9 session-scoped by default). NEVER authored in the manifest — the manifest declares only props + bindings; this $def types what the renderer materializes so the certification harness and component agree on one shape.

value.description: Current committed value from the bound scalar<number> slot; null before the slot or `default_value` sets one (empty state).

phase.description: The mandatory lifecycle state (contract §6). `loading` while the slot/tool is in flight; `empty` when bound but unset and no default_value; `ready` when a value is present; `error` on tool-execution failure or an out-of-range value with clamp=false.

out_of_range.description: true when the incoming slot value fell outside [min, max]; drives the clamp badge (text + icon) or the error state per props.clamp.

readonly.description: Resolved at merge (contract §5). true → all edit affordances disabled (aria-disabled), control still renders live.

writer.description: The slot's writer-of-record when this skill is NOT it (drives the 'Managed by {name}' tooltip / accessible description); null when this skill owns the slot.

x-hydropark-widget.$comment: NON-NORMATIVE for envelope validation (custom keyword, ignored by JSON-Schema validators). Home for the contract facets that are enforced against artifacts OUTSIDE this instance — normalization defaults, cross-artifact slot fit, runtime state, lifecycle copy, read-only behavior, and the mechanical a11y contract (contract §§2,3,5,6,8,9; SPEC 9.2–9.4/9.8/9.10). The renderer, the orchestrator's binding/merge layer, and the certification pipeline read it.

presentations.slider: Continuous horizontal track + draggable thumb; role=slider. Optional tick `marks`.
presentations.stepper: Numeric value field flanked by decrement (−) and increment (＋) buttons; role=spinbutton. Direct text entry allowed, committed on Enter/blur.

binds_state.cardinality: two-way when the owning skill is the slot's writer-of-record; otherwise read-only (contract §5).
binds_state.note: Enforced as a cross-artifact certification check (P1-04.5): the concrete slot named by binds_state must normalize to { kind:'scalar', scalar:'number' }. Binding a slider/stepper to a string/bool/enum/list/record slot blocks the combination naming the slot. Non-writers may still change the value indirectly through a tool that `writes_state` the scalar (only the writer-of-record may set it via the UI — contract §5).

reads_state.note: Extra scalar<number> slots may be observed read-only for a comparative readout (e.g. a target vs. current). An update to any observed slot re-renders the control (base.reads_state).

binds_tool.note: A committed value change may deterministically invoke the bound tool with the new value (e.g. re-scale a recipe on a servings change) with no model round-trip. Distinct from the slot write; a widget may do both — write the scalar AND fire the tool.

state_copy_defaults.$comment: Type-default copy for the mandatory states (contract §6). Overridable per-instance via base `states` (strings only — a state can never be suppressed). Localizable.

error.label: Value out of range
error.hint: Enter a number between the minimum and maximum.

read_only_behavior.renders_live: Reflects every slot update in real time — a first-class view, not a stale copy.
read_only_behavior.disables: Thumb drag, −/＋ buttons, text entry, and keyboard stepping are all inert (visually + aria-disabled/disabled).
read_only_behavior.names_owner: Exposes an accessible description / tooltip 'Managed by {writer.name}' so the user knows where edits are routed.

a11y.$comment: Mechanical a11y is guaranteed by the first-party widget-library implementation and enforced by the X-A11Y gate (axe + keyboard-nav + SR-label audit) in BOTH themes on EVERY state (contract §8). Not the skill's to author or waive; the skill may override only accessible name/description/live_region via base `a11y`.

roles.slider: role=slider with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow, aria-valuetext (value + unit_label, e.g. '4 servings'), aria-orientation=horizontal; labelled by `title`/a11y.label, described by any a11y.description and the read-only owner note.
roles.stepper: role=spinbutton with the same aria-value* set; the −/＋ controls are role=button with discrete accessible names ('Decrease {title}', 'Increase {title}'); the editable field is labelled by `title`.

keyboard.stepper_typing: type a number, commit on Enter or blur (clamped/validated per props); Escape reverts

a11y.focus_order: title/label → control (slider thumb, or stepper field) → decrement button → increment button (stepper); one Tab stop for the slider. Visible focus ring on the focused element.
a11y.disabled_extremes: At min the decrement affordance is disabled, at max the increment affordance is disabled — signalled via aria-disabled + a visual state (never color-only).
a11y.no_hue_only: Out-of-range/limit and disabled states are ALWAYS paired with text and/or an icon; `style.tone` is never the sole signal (WCAG 1.4.1).
a11y.contrast: WCAG-AA for the track, thumb, focus ring, value text, tick marks, and button glyphs, verified PER THEME (a token passing on dark may fail on light — contract §8.1).
a11y.live_region: Default polite (base a11y.live_region default): out-of-band writes (a tool changing the slot) announce the new aria-valuetext without stealing focus; direct user stepping is announced by the control's own aria-valuenow updates.
a11y.reduce_motion: Thumb/track transitions collapse under the OS reduce-motion setting (motion tokens, SPEC 9.6).
a11y.zoom: Layout and hit targets remain usable when text is scaled up (X-A11Y.3).

styling.$comment: contract §7. Skills ship no CSS: styling is only the closed `style` variant vocabulary (tone/emphasis/density/align). The widget-library CSS itself references only the --color/--space/--radius/--font/--text/--transition/--motion token namespaces (non-token-styling lint, P1-20.1).
